title: Graphorkis clavicornis
taxonomy: Graphorkis clavicornis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a part of the genus Graphorkis, which is a group of orchids native to tropical Africa. The species is further categorized under the subfamily Orchidoideae. Graphorkis clavicornis is a terrestrial orchid that grows in shaded, humid environments. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological adaptations.

intro: Graphorkis clavicornis, also known as the African Graphorkis or Forest Orchid, is a species of orchid native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unique, horn-shaped pseudobulbs. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Graphorkis clavicornis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried extract, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions in African folk medicine.
